evidence.snippet: Fireworks AI is now available first-party in Microsoft Foundry through a multi-year partnership.
evidence.text:
Fireworks AI is now available first-party in Microsoft Foundry, as part of a multi-year partnership, starting today in private preview.

Enterprise AI has a speed problem. Most infrastructure can't keep up with how fast open models are moving. New architectures, fine-tuned weights, models that aren't on anyone's approved list yet. The usual answer was "manage your own serving infrastructure." That's a real cost.

Fireworks runs the inference. Azure owns the governance. Your weights stay yours.

Beyond custom weights, teams can also access the latest open models on day zero. No waiting for the marketplace to catch up. Serverless inference for prototyping, and a path to production that doesn't require re-architecting anything.

Fireworks handles the serving layer. Azure handles the rest. Your boss handles the congratulations.

Want in → https://t.co/HJju3f0lNR

evidence.snippet: The Fireworks AI Experimentation Platform and Build SDK are launching.
evidence.text:
🔥 Launching the Fireworks AI Experimentation Platform (GA) and Build SDK (Beta)🔥

Top AI teams don't win by chance - they win by running 10X more experiments than competitors.

Launching early, collecting feedback, tuning and selecting models, running offline evals, and validating impact with product metrics in a fast, continuous cycle is critical to build successful AI products.

But, the need to manage complex infrastructure, wait for GPUs, reconcile differences between training and serving, and maintain reliability, slow AI teams down.

Now, with the Firework’s experimentation platform, experimenting with open models just got a whole lot easier. With the help of our brand-new Python SDK (in beta), you can:

→ Stop waiting for GPUs, fine-tune and deploy 1000s of models on a single GPU deployment using Multi-LoRA

→ Programmatically run large-scale fine-tuning experiments

→ Scale evals with fully managed and autoscaling GPU deployments

Check out our blog post to learn more and see a demo of running 125 experiments in 3 minutes on a single deployment: https://t.co/MmNfki7n9g

evidence.snippet: The public preview is open now and can be started for free.
evidence.text:
🚀 Fireworks Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT) launched! 

After many months of iteration with real world use cases, we are excited to launch Fireworks RFT public preview. It’s a managed RL service that turns open frontier models (e.g. DeepSeek V3, Kimi K2) into custom agents for tools, code & reasoning.

Our design principle is to bring RL to integrate directly with your agents in production, instead of you simulating or redesigning your agents to do RL.  Fireworks RFT trains on full agent trajectories (multi-step, tools, retries), so the model learns your workflow in production. 

Here are real-world results:

👉 Genspark Deep research agent → +10% quality vs SOTA closed model, +33% tool calls, ~50% lower cost
👉 Vercel V0 coding agent → +33% better than closed models, 93–94% error-free, 40× faster.

🔥 Fireworks RFT public preview is open now  - start training today for free 👇
 https://t.co/cRhz9IXuMO

current_output.title: Latency stayed stable even as tasks extended across multiple steps.

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: Latency stayed stable even as tasks extended across multiple steps.
evidence.text:
We ran 720 browser agent tasks with @nottecore across frontier models. 

One baseline model produced malformed outputs in ~1 out of every 5 calls, leading to retries inside multi-step workflows. 

Across Kimi K2.5, GLM-5, and MiniMax M2.5 served on Fireworks, retry rates were near zero and latency stayed stable even as tasks extended across multiple steps.

Same workload. Same agent loop. Different execution behavior. 

That gap is what shows up as cost, latency, and reliability divergence in production agent systems.

Read the report: https://t.co/6thZVvLomR
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

MiniMax-M1-80k is now available on Fireworks!

Why it matters:
➡️ Extreme long-context reasoning – Natively supports 1 million tokens (8× more than DeepSeek R1). Ideal for use cases like multi-file code refactoring, legal document analysis, and multi-turn agent workflows.
➡️ Efficient compute – Its hybrid MoE + lightning attention architecture uses up to 75% fewer FLOPs for long generations compared to peers, making it both fast and cost-effective.
➡️ Elite benchmark performance – MiniMax-M1-80k outperforms other leading open models on tough tasks:

- Software engineering (SWE-bench)
- Tool use (TAU-bench)
- Long-context understanding (MRCR, LongBench)

evidence.snippet: The stack is described as low-latency, high-throughput, and cost-efficient.
evidence.text:
We just published a new blog and open-source demo on Building Enterprise-Scale RAG Systems with Fireworks AI + MongoDB Atlas

Many companies today are overwhelmed by unstructured data - from earnings calls and financial reports to legal documents and internal PDFs. Making sense of it all is a growing challenge.

How do you actually extract insights at speed and scale?

That’s where Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) steps in, and we’ve built an enterprise-ready system from scratch to show you how.

What’s inside:
🔍 Real-time semantic search across PDFs, DOCX, audio & more
🧠 Whisper V3 Turbo for 20x faster audio transcription
⚡ Fireworks AI+ MongoDB Atlas powering low-latency, high-throughput, cost-efficient inference
🔐 Transparency, confidence scores, and traceability built in
🤖 Built to scale with multi-agent orchestration, table parsing, and cross-company benchmarking in the roadmap

If you're working on enterprise AI or building search systems that actually understand context, check it out: https://t.co/gB56qIxoQo

All code, docs, and tests are open source → https://t.co/6wTxAJLErY
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: The engineering work keeps the training overhead small enough to be practical, with only about 7 percent slower training for four lanes.
evidence.text:
Everyone is talking about @deepseek_ai's mHC (Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections) paper; let’s break it down in simple language.

What they’re trying to do: A model has one “main highway,” the residual stream, which carries information through all layers. DeepSeek splits that highway into a few parallel lanes so different lanes can carry different kinds of information, and the model can merge that traffic as it goes.

The problem: If every layer is allowed to freely “turn the knobs” on how much each lane is amplified, those knobs can compound over multiple layers. 
The result: Some lanes get too much traffic, others remain empty, and training becomes unstable.

DeepSeek added a simple rule: each layer can redistribute information across lanes but not amplify it overall, thereby conserving the information flow. To mix our metaphors slightly, think of it as a mixer board. You can move sound between channels, but you can’t secretly crank the overall volume. 

The non-obvious win: This rule holds even when stacking many layers. Instead of small mistakes that add up and explode, you get stable mixing all the way down, while still benefiting from multiple lanes.

What you get in practice: training becomes much more predictable, and quality improves across benchmarks (final loss reduction of 0.021 vs the baseline on a 27B LLM).

Why it matters for real systems: This kind of multi-lane design can be expensive to run. DeepSeek also did engineering work on custom kernels and a smarter backward pass, so it’s only ~7 percent slower to train with four lanes, which is surprisingly practical.

Net: More expressive residual connections, without the usual instability, and the overhead is small enough to actually use.

This is really impressive research. We’re excited to see the next generation of models built on top of this architecture. In the meantime, if you’re ready to try today’s DeepSeek models (and other frontier open weight models), you can get started with one click. You can run them on the Fireworks Inference Cloud, which delivers open-source models at blazing speed, optimized for your use case, and scaled globally: https://t.co/e8syugMkdp
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: For long-horizon coding agents, a clean 1M window and stable infrastructure are better than leaderboard screenshots.
evidence.text:
You may have seen other platforms announce day-zero support within minutes of weights dropping, but there is a key difference between inference providers and routers. 

Routers forward your GLM 5.2 calls to someone else’s endpoint (typically the model lab’s own API). 

Fireworks runs the weights directly: end‑to‑end serving path, zero data retention, production‑grade latency. 

For long‑horizon coding agents, a clean 1M window + stable infra beats leaderboard screenshots.
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

evidence.author: lqiao
evidence.snippet: The runtime is described as having negligible quality tradeoff.
evidence.text:
🔥 FireAttention v2:  speed and long context, pick two! 🔥

At @FireworksAI_HQ , we challenge ourselves constantly -- can we develop a dramatically more efficient serving for LLMs with RAG in a long context window with negligible quality tradeoff? 

We built our next generation runtime, FireAttention v2. When compared with vLLM, the Fireworks stack now serves Qwen2 and Mixtral (32k context window) up to ~12x faster and ~8x higher throughput. 

For technical details, check our blogpost: https://t.co/sXlg75ac24
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @lqiao

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: It creates a safer way to iterate on prompts, models, and features without regressions.
evidence.text:
How do you go from a blank project to your first fully tested AI agent?

In our latest blog, we share how we adapted Test-Driven Development (TDD) into the LLM era, using evals to define desired behaviors before writing code.

By supercharging Claude Code with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, we grounded the agent in the right context (docs, repos, wikis).

✔️ Faster setup of evals with Eval Protocol
✔️ Richer, more reliable test suites generated automatically
✔️ A safer workflow for iterating on prompts, models, and features without regressions

This shift turns AI into a true development partner- expanding tests, validating behaviors, and helping engineers scale agent capabilities with confidence.

👉 Read the full blog here: https://t.co/D2cBvDlGio
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: It provides a visual, interactive way to slice and dice evals across models, prompts, runs, scores, parameters, and more.
evidence.text:
What if you could explore your AI eval results like an Excel pivot table, without writing a single line of code?

Meet Pivot View in Eval Protocol.

A visual, interactive way to slice and dice your evals across models, prompts, runs, scores, parameters, and more.

→ Want to know which model performed best?
→ Which prompt worked consistently across test cases?
→ Whether increasing temperature helped or hurt?
→ If your fine-tuning actually improved results?
→ Where your model fails most often?

You can answer all of these in seconds.
No scripts. No dashboards. Just clarity.

It’s designed for fast iteration and flexible analysis- perfect for AI engineers and product teams shipping models in the real world.

📊 Try it out here: https://t.co/S0yf9ewACm
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: Transparency, confidence scores, and traceability are built in.
evidence.text:
We just published a new blog and open-source demo on Building Enterprise-Scale RAG Systems with Fireworks AI + MongoDB Atlas

Many companies today are overwhelmed by unstructured data - from earnings calls and financial reports to legal documents and internal PDFs. Making sense of it all is a growing challenge.

How do you actually extract insights at speed and scale?

That’s where Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) steps in, and we’ve built an enterprise-ready system from scratch to show you how.

What’s inside:
🔍 Real-time semantic search across PDFs, DOCX, audio & more
🧠 Whisper V3 Turbo for 20x faster audio transcription
⚡ Fireworks AI+ MongoDB Atlas powering low-latency, high-throughput, cost-efficient inference
🔐 Transparency, confidence scores, and traceability built in
🤖 Built to scale with multi-agent orchestration, table parsing, and cross-company benchmarking in the roadmap

If you're working on enterprise AI or building search systems that actually understand context, check it out: https://t.co/gB56qIxoQo

All code, docs, and tests are open source → https://t.co/6wTxAJLErY
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

Training trillion-parameter MoEs is an infra problem disguised as a modeling problem.

So we built the infra solution. Cursor used it to train Composer 2. Now it's available for Kimi K2.5, Qwen3.5 397B, MiniMax M2.5, and more:

→Fused RL loss (~2x faster PPO)
→MXFP8 expert kernels on Blackwell
→Composable 4D parallelism
→1M+ token context training validated

Here's how it all works ↓
https://t.co/PA20I8EFaD

evidence.snippet: Its streaming transcription beats DeepGram Nova in noisy environments.
evidence.text:
🔥 Day 2/7 -- launching Fireworks Voice Agent Platform 🔥

Today, we’re launching Voice Agent Platform (Beta) on @FireworksAI_HQ   — a developer-first stack to build interactive, production-grade voice agents.

🧠 What makes it different?
Sub-500ms latency: Our proprietary ASR, TTS, and LLMs are co-located and optimized for parallel, real-time performance.

Full-stack customization: Swap in any LLM (fine-tuned or base), customize pronunciation via IPA, and customize every component to your use case.

One provider, zero complexity: Replace 3+ vendors with a single platform that handles ASR, LLM, TTS, and infra, end to end.

We’ve built everything from scratch: streaming transcription that beats DeepGram Nova in noisy environments, steerable TTS with 40+ voices, and natural conversation flows with end-of-utterance detection, interrupt handling, and native function calling.

Watch the demos 
https://t.co/jSUY0sSUfE
https://t.co/mUGluab8ww

Detailed blogpost: https://t.co/BerjW38zBD

try it now at https://t.co/KOOlFBtEpN
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @lqiao

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: The low reasoning_effort setting improves response times for latency-sensitive applications.
evidence.text:
Faster Reasoning– Now Live with DeepSeek-R1 on Fireworks AI! 🔥

We’re expanding reasoning_effort controls with the latest DeepSeek-R1 model, allowing developers to optimize computational efficiency while maintaining strong task performance.

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁’𝘀 𝗡𝗲𝘄?

✔️ reasoning_effort = low: Limits Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning to 40% of full length, improving response times for latency-sensitive applications.
✔️ Benchmark Performance: Achieves 63% accuracy on AIME 2024 math competition problems, outperforming o1-mini_low (60%).
✔️ Customizable Effort Levels: Developers can fine-tune reasoning depth by setting reasoning_effort = [integer < 20,000], directly controlling computational limits.

𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗗𝗲𝘁𝗮𝗶𝗹𝘀

→ Faster, Focused Outputs: Shorter CoT paths reduce unnecessary reasoning overhead.
→ Hard Cutoff for CoT: The model auto-terminates reasoning with an </think> token at the defined effort limit.
→Minimized Unproductive Loops: Conditional logprob adjustments discourage unnecessary deliberation.
→Backward Compatible: Existing workflows using reasoning_effort = medium remain unaffected.

𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝗨𝘀𝗲 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘀𝗼𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴_𝗲𝗳𝗳𝗼𝗿𝘁 = 𝗹𝗼𝘄?

↳ Optimized for latency-sensitive, cost-aware applications.
↳ Reduces unnecessary processing while maintaining competitive accuracy.
↳ Streamlined integration—no prompt tuning required, just a single parameter update.

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁’𝘀 𝗡𝗲𝘅𝘁?

We’re exploring reasoning_effort = high for applications requiring extended reasoning depth. More updates coming soon.

𝗚𝗲𝘁 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗲𝗱 ↓

Set reasoning_effort in your API call:

response = https://t.co/JoFFm7Wpfc.completions.create(
model="fireworks/deepseek-r1",
reasoning_effort="low"
)

--------

Try DeepSeek R1 models on Fireworks AI: https://t.co/Aef6IWCZZd

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: It claims about 38% fewer hallucinations than V3, making outputs more consistent.
evidence.text:
Last week, we introduced DeepSeek V3.1 on Fireworks AI.
It’s a meaningful step forward compared to the previous V3 version, designed with real-world applications in mind.

Here’s what’s new:

 ✨ Hybrid reasoning modes: Toggle between “thinking” (chain-of-thought) for deeper logic and “non-thinking” for faster replies.
 📉 Lesser Hallucinations: ~38% fewer hallucinations compared to V3, making outputs more consistent.
 📚 128K context window: Trained with 10× more long-context data, enabling analysis of large documents end-to-end.
 🌍 Multilingual support: Across 100+ languages, including improvements in Asian and low-resource languages.
 🤖 Better tool + agent workflows: Improved function calling, memory, and API integration.

Early adopters are already building with V3.1 for research copilots, enterprise workflows, and global assistants.

Check out our detailed blog about DeepSeek v3.1 here: https://t.co/FgtUbCKYsD 

Find link in comments to start building with DeepSeek V3.1 👇
evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: The method was validated against the official Tau Bench Airline benchmark and showed a strong correlation.
evidence.text:
Stop guessing which LLM is best for your use case!
Public benchmarks don't reflect the real-world trade-offs between cost, speed, and quality on your data. The real answer is already in your production logs.

Our new blog post, "Traces Are All You Need" shows you how to turn your existing observability data into a powerful, data-driven model leaderboard in minutes.

We demonstrate a fast, label-free method using an LLM judge and our open-source toolkit, Eval Protocol. 

The best part? We validated it against the official Tau Bench Airline benchmark and found a strong correlation.

This means you can:
✅ Rapidly iterate and test new models.
✅ Get a trustworthy signal for model quality on your specific use case.
✅ Make smarter engineering and business decisions.

Read the full post here: https://t.co/749xj1dleU

For a clear and practical view of where retrieval is heading, watch the full episode on our YouTube channel: https://t.co/WaAseHGo1f

More Fireside Chats coming soon.

supported_claims: Fireworks AI provides a data-driven, label-free method to benchmark LLMs using production logs and observability data, validated against official benchmarks.
Fireworks AI is integrated first-party in Microsoft Foundry, enabling serverless inference with governance by Azure and access to the latest open models from day zero.
Fireworks AI introduces AIML, a prompt-based language and runtime that simplifies building and shipping enterprise-grade AI agents without requiring deep AI expertise.
Fireworks AI has been recognized by industry leaders and lists such as Redpoint's InfraRed 100 and Forbes Cloud 100, highlighting its foundational role in AI infrastructure.
Fireworks AI supports day-0 access to cutting-edge models like GLM 4.7 and Deep Cogito’s state-of-the-art open LLMs, which outperform leading open models in reasoning and coding benchmarks.
Fireworks AI emphasizes continuous improvement of domain-specific AI agents through tight feedback loops rather than static fine-tuned models.
Fireworks AI targets the enterprise AI speed problem by providing infrastructure that keeps pace with rapidly evolving open models and custom weights.
what_not_to_claim: No direct user pain points or negative feedback are provided in this marketing evidence.
No detailed technical limitations or challenges of Fireworks AI are mentioned.
No explicit competitive disadvantages or customer churn reasons are indicated.

supported_claims: Fast iteration and speed are key product features that drive deep user engagement in generative AI products.
Fun and fast interaction are important for viral adoption and user retention in AI-native creative tools.
Fireworks AI launched a Voice Agent Platform (Beta) that offers sub-500ms latency with proprietary ASR, TTS, and LLMs optimized for real-time performance.
The Voice Agent Platform supports full-stack customization allowing developers to swap LLMs and customize pronunciation and components for specific use cases.
Fireworks AI provides an integrated, end-to-end platform replacing multiple vendors for ASR, LLM, TTS, and infrastructure, simplifying complexity for developers.
what_not_to_claim: Specific user pain points or feature requests from end users.
Competitive comparisons beyond the stated product capabilities.

### Serverless

### On-demand Deployment

Built for long-horizon agent loops.

evidence.snippet: Kimi K2 Thinking reasons step by step while dynamically invoking tools.
evidence.text:
Kimi K2 Thinking API & Playground | Fireworks AI

GLM 5.2 is live! Opus-level intelligence at open-source rates. Pay per token on serverless. Try it today.

Model Library/Moonshot AI/Kimi K2 Thinking

# Kimi K2 Thinking

Ready

Deploy on Demand

Fine-tune

model path:accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2-thinking

Kimi K2 Thinking is the latest, most capable version of open-source thinking model. Starting with Kimi K2, it was built as a thinking agent that reasons step-by-step while dynamically invoking tools. It sets a new state-of-the-art on Humanity's Last Exam (HLE), BrowseComp, and other benchmarks by dramatically scaling multi-step reasoning depth and maintaining stable tool-use across 200–300 sequential calls. At the same time, K2 Thinking is a native INT4 quantization model with 256k context window, achieving lossless reductions in inference latency and GPU memory usage.

## Kimi K2 Thinking API Features

### Fine-tuning

Docs

Kimi K2 Thinking can be customized with your data to improve responses. Fireworks uses LoRA to efficiently train and deploy your personalized model

### On-demand Deployment

Docs

On-demand deployments allow you to use Kimi K2 Thinking on dedicated GPUs with Fireworks' high-performance serving stack with high reliability and no rate limits.

## Kimi K2 Thinking FAQs

What is Kimi K2 Thinking and who developed it?

Kimi K2 Thinking is the latest version of Moonshot AI's open-source “thinking model,” designed for advanced reasoning tasks. It interleaves step-by-step chain-of-thought reasoning with autonomous tool use, achieving strong performance across benchmarks like HLE, AIME25, and BrowseComp.

What applications and use cases does Kimi K2 Thinking excel at?

Kimi K2 Thinking is optimized for:

- •Agentic systems and tool-augmented reasoning
- •Coding (SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, OJ-Bench)
- •Autonomous search (BrowseComp, FinSearchComp-T3)
- •Longform writing and conversational AI
- •Enterprise RAG and complex reasoning tasks like AIME25, GPQA

What is the maximum context length for Kimi K2 Thinking?

The maximum context length is 256k tokens.

What is the usable context window for Kimi K2 Thinking?

The usable context is 256K tokens. The model maintains coherence across long sequences and supports up to 200–300 sequential tool-use steps without degradation.

Does Kimi K2 Thinking support quantized formats (4-bit/8-bit)?

Yes. Kimi K2 Thinking is natively trained for INT4 quantization using Quantization-Aware Training (QAT), enabling lossless performance and up to 2x faster generation.

What is the default temperature of Kimi K2 Thinking on Fireworks AI?

The recommended default temperature is 1.0.

What is the maximum output length Fireworks allows for Kimi K2 Thinking?

Fireworks allows a maximum output of 4096 tokens per completion by default.

Does Kimi K2 Thinking support function-calling schemas?

Yes. It supports OpenAI-style function-calling, and developers must provide a list of tools for each request.

How many parameters does Kimi K2 Thinking have?

The model has 1 trillion total parameters, with 32 billion active parameters per forward pass using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with 384 experts, 8 selected per token.

How are tokens counted (prompt vs completion)?

Tokens are metered as input (prompt) and output (completion) separately. Fireworks charges per 1M tokens input/output.

What license governs commercial use of Kimi K2 Thinking?

Kimi K2 Thinking is released under a Modified MIT License, permitting commercial use with some additional terms.

evidence.title: Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ

evidence.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence.snippet: It is described as having top-tier coding and reasoning capabilities.
evidence.text:
Qwen 3 is now live on Fireworks AI!

Qwen 3 is a SOTA open-weights model for building AI agents:

➡️ SOTA agentic tool use: Highest ranked open model on BFCL (tool-calling) benchmark, below gpt4-2024-11-20 and above gpt4.5. Tuned to support Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools with the Qwen-Agent framework for multi-step tool usage (function calling), browser actions, and code execution. Fireworks support function calling for Qwen 3 models.

➡️ Top-tier coding and reasoning capabilities: Outperforms many open and closed models on LiveCodeBench, and only three thousands coders on CodeForces are better at competitive coding than Qwen3.

➡️ Hybrid reasoning with enable_thinking: Qwen 3 introduces a dynamic soft switch. You can control whether the model reasons step-by-step (/think) or responds instantly (/no_think), even within the same conversation.

➡️ Massive long-context capabilities: Native 32K token context window (with verified support beyond 130K tokens), perfect for full codebases, research archives, and large chat histories.

➡️True multilingual reach: Trained on 36 trillion tokens across 119 languages and dialects, which delivers strong multilingual instruction-following and generation.

➡️MoE efficiency advantage: Activates only a fraction of experts per token, delivering strong reasoning quality with significantly lower compute and memory requirements compared to dense models of similar total size.

➡️ Fully open-weight under Apache 2.0 license: Fine-tune, customize, and deploy freely with no restrictions.

👉 Try Qwen 3 now on the Fireworks Model Playground https://t.co/dMAPrSrioy

👉 Or integrate it directly via our OpenAI-compatible API

Whether you’re building enterprise copilots, agentic systems, or next-gen search, Qwen 3 gives you unprecedented control over thinking depth, speed, and cost.

evidence.text:
What if you could explore your AI eval results like an Excel pivot table, without writing a single line of code?

Meet Pivot View in Eval Protocol.

A visual, interactive way to slice and dice your evals across models, prompts, runs, scores, parameters, and more.

→ Want to know which model performed best?
→ Which prompt worked consistently across test cases?
→ Whether increasing temperature helped or hurt?
→ If your fine-tuning actually improved results?
→ Where your model fails most often?

You can answer all of these in seconds.
No scripts. No dashboards. Just clarity.

It’s designed for fast iteration and flexible analysis- perfect for AI engineers and product teams shipping models in the real world.

📊 Try it out here: https://t.co/S0yf9ewACm

source_payload.task: Rewrite the insight using only this evidence.

expected_checks: Returns evidence IDs or URLs.
Separates official, employee, creator, competitor, and organic sources when present.
Does not summarize away source provenance.
turns.user: Show the raw evidence and identify who is speaking in each source.

expected_checks: Final category should be compatible with: marketing_narrative
Official-only evidence must not become pain point or feature request.
turns.user: Classify whether this is user feedback, marketing narrative, competitive evidence, or off-topic.

expected_checks: No unsupported buyer/user claims.
Includes what not to claim or equivalent guardrails when evidence is promotional.
Keeps source-role awareness in the answer.

expected_output.description: Official posts emphasize Kimi K2 Thinking's strong coding, multi-step reasoning, and tool-use capabilities, including a large 256k context window and real-time Chain of Thought reasoning, positioning it as ideal for complex autonomous agents. However, these communications do not specify safe context lengths, failure modes, or necessary guardrails for long agent loops, leaving potential users without clear boundaries for dependable autonomous production use.
expected_output.recommended_action: Provide clearer documentation or marketing content that outlines Kimi K2 Thinking's operational limits, recommended context lengths, and scenarios requiring guardrails to aid teams in assessing reliability for long-horizon autonomous agents.
expected_output.title: Kimi K2 Thinking marketing highlights strengths but lacks detailed guidance on reliability limits for long-horizon agents
